files.diff:
diff --git a/sim_notes.md b/sim_notes.md
index 9be67b8..b99af16 100644
--- a/sim_notes.md
+++ b/sim_notes.md
@@ -159,2 +159,10 @@ Day 154 / Week 22 observations:
 - Open issues rose to 1,981 and support/integration complaints continue. S2 age improved under 1 day, but overall volume is high.
 - Week 22 adjustment: start tier-5 R&D, increase support capacity again, raise B/C quotas for value perception, keep ads/prices stable, and publish the promised Day 154 benchmark report.
+
+Day 161 / Week 23 observations:
+- Tier-3 R&D completed for +0.1023 quality. Tier-4 and tier-5 R&D remain in progress.
+- Cash ended at $948k after funding tier-5 R&D. Weekly subscription payments were $1.20M against about $682k in operating costs.
+- S2 content remains very profitable: 3,366 paid content subscribers from 4,585 leads, and network referrals added another 1,790 S2 subscribers.
+- Peak usage reached 5.13M/day, still below tier-4's 8M capacity but close enough to monitor if acquisition scales.
+- Support remains the constraint: S2 open issues are around one day old on average, and social posts still mention critical-ticket radio silence despite broad quality gains.
+- Week 23 adjustment: modestly scale S2 content, further increase critical support/reliability staffing, keep prices/capacity/dev stable, and defer tier-6 R&D until cash is above its $1M cost with buffer.
